code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, EmailStr
from typing import Optional, List
from datetime import date

class Product(BaseModel):
    product_id: int = Field(..., gt=0, description="Unique identifier for the product")
    name: str = Field(..., min_length=3, max_length=100, description="Name of the product")
    description: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=500, description="Detailed description of the product")
    price: float = Field(..., gt=0.0, description="Price of the product")
    currency: str = Field("USD", pattern=r"^[A-Z]{3}$", description="Currency code (e.g., USD, EUR)")
    in_stock: bool = Field(True, description="Whether the product is currently in stock")
    tags: List[str] = Field([], description="List of tags associated with the product")
    created_at: date = Field(default_factory=date.today, description="Date the product was added")

class Customer(BaseModel):
    customer_id: int = Field(..., gt=0, description="Unique identifier for the customer")
    first_name: str = Field(..., min_length=2, max_length=50, description="Customer's first name")
    last_name: str = Field(..., min_length=2, max_length=50, description="Customer's last name")
    email: EmailStr = Field(..., description="Customer's email address")
    phone_number: Optional[str] = Field(None, pattern=r"^\+?[0-9\s\-()]{7,20}$", description="Customer's phone number")
    address: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=200, description="Customer's physical address")

class Order(BaseModel):
    order_id: int = Field(..., gt=0, description="Unique identifier for the order")
    customer: Customer = Field(..., description="Details of the customer placing the order")
    products_ordered: List[Product] = Field(..., min_items=1, description="List of products in the order")
    total_amount: float = Field(..., gt=0.0, description="Total amount of the order")
    order_date: date = Field(default_factory=date.today, description="Date the order was placed")
    status: str = Field("pending", pattern=r"^(pending|processing|shipped|delivered|cancelled)$", description="Current status of the order")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example Usage and Validation
    try:
        # Valid product
        p1 = Product(
            product_id=1,
            name="Laptop Pro X",
            description="Powerful laptop for professionals",
            price=1200.50,
            tags=["electronics", "laptop"]
        )
        print("Valid Product:", p1.model_dump_json(indent=2))

        # Invalid product (price, name length)
        # p_invalid = Product(
        #     product_id=2,
        #     name="X", # Too short
        #     price=0.0, # Not greater than 0
        #     currency="ABC"
        # )
        # print(p_invalid)

        # Valid customer
        c1 = Customer(
            customer_id=101,
            first_name="Alice",
            last_name="Smith",
            email="alice.smith@example.com",
            phone_number="+1-555-123-4567"
        )
        print("\nValid Customer:", c1.model_dump_json(indent=2))

        # Invalid customer (email format)
        # c_invalid = Customer(
        #     customer_id=102,
        #     first_name="Bob",
        #     last_name="Johnson",
        #     email="bob.johnson@invalid"
        # )
        # print(c_invalid)

        # Valid order
        o1 = Order(
            order_id=2001,
            customer=c1,
            products_ordered=[p1],
            total_amount=1200.50
        )
        print("\nValid Order:", o1.model_dump_json(indent=2))

        # Invalid order (empty products_ordered)
        # o_invalid = Order(
        #     order_id=2002,
        #     customer=c1,
        #     products_ordered=[], # min_items=1
        #     total_amount=10.0
        # )
        # print(o_invalid)

    except Exception as e:
        print("\nValidation Error:", e)
